definition:
1. of  or for the council  or the councillors , (Xenophon Historicus) 
2. able to advise  or deliberate , (Plato Philosophus), etc. 
3. as Substantive, βουλευτικόν, in the Athen. theatre, the seats next the orchestra, reserved for the Council , (Aristophanes Comicus) 
4. the senatorial order , (Plutarch) (ML)
